title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: can install readapra CRAN using: can install development version GitHub using remotes package:

title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: readapra package facilitates downloading importing statistical publications published APRA. package also allows importing data locally saved statistical publication files. Currently readapra package supports downloading importing Authorised Deposit-taking Institution (ADI) statistical publications. intention future versions readapra also accommodate APRA’s general insurance, life insurance friendly societies, private health insurance superannuation statistical publications.

title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: following ADI statistical publications can downloaded imported readapra: Quarterly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Performance Statistics (QADIPS) Quarterly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Centralised Publication (QADICP) Quarterly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Property Exposures Statistics (QADIPEXS) (current historic series) Monthly Authorised Deposit- taking Institution Statistics (MADIS) (current historic series) Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Points Presence Statistics (ADIPOPS)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: ability download import APRA’s statistical publications readapra greatly eases analysis visualisation data via packages ggplot2. following example demonstrates plotting total resident assets monthly basis Australia’s four major banks. start first librarying required packages: download import Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Institution Statistics (MADIS) data using read_apra() function: filter clean-desired data like : finally can plot data using ggplot2:
code: library(readapra) library(tidyverse) madis_data <- read_apra(stat_pub = "madis", cur_hist = "current") major_bank_assets <-    madis_data %>%   filter(     abn %in% c(48123123124, 33007457141, 12004044937, 11005357522),     series == "Total residents loans and finance leases"   ) %>%   mutate(     short_name = dplyr::case_when(       abn == 48123123124 ~ "CBA",       abn == 33007457141 ~ "WBC",       abn == 12004044937 ~ "NAB",       abn == 11005357522 ~ "ANZ",     )   ) %>%   group_by(short_name) %>%   mutate(value = 100 * value / first(value)) %>%   ungroup() ggplot(   data = major_bank_assets,   mapping = aes(date, value, colour = short_name) ) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(     title = "Major Australian Banks' Total Resident Loans and Finance Leases",     subtitle = "Indexed to March 2019",     caption = "Source: APRA"   ) +   theme_bw() +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     legend.position = "top",     legend.title = element_blank(),     axis.title = element_blank(),     axis.text = element_text(colour = "black")    ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(     "CBA" = "#FECA0A",     "WBC" = "#DF0000",     "NAB" = "#000000",     "ANZ" = "#009FE1"   ))

title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: Corporate networks, especially using Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), may restrict ability download files within R session. possible fix utilise "wininet" method downloading files. Users can specify "wininet" method (download method) readapra use setting "R_READAPRA_DL_METHOD" environment variable. set "R_READAPRA_DL_METHOD" environment variable "wininet" current session, use following code: can add "R_READAPRA_DL_METHOD" = "wininet" .Renviron file ensure download setting persists across R sessions. can conveniently access edit .Renviron file usethis package:

title: Download and Tidy Data from the Australian Prudential Regulation Authority
text: package affiliated endorsed Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (APRA). data provided subject conditions restrictions set APRA website.

what: Statistical Publications Produced by APRA — apra_stat_pubs
title: Statistical Publications Produced by APRA — apra_stat_pubs
text: dataset contains record statistical publications produced APRA available readapra.

title: Statistical Publications Produced by APRA — apra_stat_pubs
Statistical Publications Produced by APRA — apra_stat_pubs
text: data frame 7 rows 3 variables: stat_pub_name full name statistical publication. stat_pub_acronym acronym statistical publication. cur_hist available current historical versions statistical publication.

what: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
title: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
text: Download statistical publication file APRA's website. default files saved temporary directory.

title: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
code: download_apra(   stat_pub,   cur_hist = "current",   path = tempdir(),   overwrite = TRUE,   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
text: stat_pub character vector detailing statistical publication downloaded. Must match valid value apra_stat_pubs_acronym variable apra_stat_pubs dataset. cur_hist character vector detailing whether download current historic statistical publication. Must match valid value cur_hist variable apra_stat_pubs dataset. path path downloaded file saved. Uses base::tempdir() default. overwrite whether overwrite previously downloaded statistical publication file re-running function. quiet whether suppress download progress bar. ... additional arguments passed utils::download.file().

title: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
text: character vector detailing file path downloaded file.

title: Download a Statistical Publication File from APRA's Website — download_apra
code: # \donttest{ # Download a statistical publication file: download_path <-   download_apra(stat_pub = "qadips", cur_hist = "current")  # View the file path of the statistical publication file: print(download_path) #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpcKLgiu/Quarterly%20authorised%20deposit-taking%20institution%20performance-September%202004%20to%20December%202025.xlsx" # }

title: Read APRA's Statistical Publications — read_apra
text: Download import specific statistical publication produced APRA. Please consult apra_stat_pubs dataset see APRA's statistical publications available readapra.

title: Read APRA's Statistical Publications — read_apra
code: read_apra(   stat_pub,   cur_hist = "current",   path = tempdir(),   quiet = FALSE,   overwrite = TRUE,   ... )

title: Read APRA's Statistical Publications — read_apra
text: stat_pub character vector detailing statistical publication downloaded imported. Must match valid value apra_stat_pubs_acronym variable apra_stat_pubs dataset. cur_hist character vector detailing whether download import current historic statistical publication. Must match valid value cur_hist variable apra_stat_pubs dataset. path path downloaded file saved. Uses base::tempdir() default. quiet whether suppress download progress bar. overwrite whether overwrite previously downloaded statistical publication file re-running function. ... additional arguments passed utils::download.file().

title: Read APRA's Statistical Publications — read_apra
text: tibble containing statistical publication data.

title: Read APRA's Statistical Publications — read_apra
code: # \donttest{ # Download and import the current MADIS data: current_madis_data <-   read_apra(stat_pub = "madis", cur_hist = "current")  # Examine the current MADIS data: print(current_madis_data) #> # A tibble: 287,550 × 9 #>    statistics_publication_name               date           abn institution_name #>    <chr>                                     <date>       <dbl> <chr>            #>  1 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  2 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  3 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  4 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  5 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  6 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  7 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  8 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  9 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #> 10 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #> # ℹ 287,540 more rows #> # ℹ 5 more variables: balance_sheet_category <chr>, series <chr>, #> #   frequency <chr>, unit <chr>, value <dbl> # }

what: Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
title: Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
text: Import local file specific statistical publication produced APRA. Please consult apra_stat_pubs dataset see APRA's statistical publications available readapra.

title: Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
text: file_path path local file statistical publication data imported. stat_pub character vector detailing statistical publication imported. Must match valid value apra_stat_pubs_acronym variable apra_stat_pubs dataset. cur_hist character vector detailing whether import current historic statistical publication. Must match valid value cur_hist variable apra_stat_pubs dataset.

title: Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
text: tibble containing statistical publication data.

title: Read APRA Publication Statistics Locally — read_apra_local
code: # \donttest{ # Download the current MADIS data and get the file path: current_madis_file_path <-   download_apra(stat_pub = "madis", cur_hist = "current")  # Import the current MADIS data: current_madis_data <-   read_apra_local(     stat_pub = "madis",     cur_hist = "current",     file_path = current_madis_file_path   )  # Examine the current MADIS data: print(current_madis_data) #> # A tibble: 287,550 × 9 #>    statistics_publication_name               date           abn institution_name #>    <chr>                                     <date>       <dbl> <chr>            #>  1 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  2 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  3 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  4 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  5 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  6 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  7 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  8 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #>  9 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #> 10 Monthly Authorised Deposit-taking Instit… 2019-03-31 7.07e10 ABN AMRO Bank N… #> # ℹ 287,540 more rows #> # ℹ 5 more variables: balance_sheet_category <chr>, series <chr>, #> #   frequency <chr>, unit <chr>, value <dbl> # }

title: readapra 0.2.3
text: ABN field MADIS, QADICP ADIPOPS datasets now always numeric.

title: readapra 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-22 Removed dependency polite, robotstxt, ratelimitr memoise. returned ADIPOPS, MADIS QADICP tibbles now sorted date.

title: readapra 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-02-22 Fixed issue inconsistent formatting cause multiplication data. Removed usage native pipe operator (|>).

title: readapra 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-01-21 Replaced read_x() read_x_local() style functions read_apra() read_apra_local(). Added download_apra() function. Added apra_stat_pubs dataset.

title: readapra 0.1.1
text: Removed dontrun status examples documentation. Added link APRA’s website package description.

title: readapra 0.1.0
text: read_x() read_x_local() style functions added APRA’s Authorised Deposit-taking Institution statistical publications.
